schema.appDescription: Plan for early retirement at any age. Calculate corpus needed, monthly investment, and post-retirement income to sustain expenses until life expectancy.

howToSteps.name: Enter your details
howToSteps.text: Fill in the input fields for the Early Retirement Calculator.

howToSteps.name: View instant results
howToSteps.text: Results update in real time as you type.

howToSteps.name: Explore breakdown
howToSteps.text: Open the year-by-year breakdown to see numbers over time.

howToSteps.name: Compare scenarios
howToSteps.text: Adjust inputs to compare scenarios and optimise your decision.

faq.q: What is the difference between early retirement and FIRE?
faq.a: FIRE (Financial Independence, Retire Early) and early retirement are related concepts. FIRE emphasises financial independence first — you could work but choose not to. Early retirement focuses on leaving employment before the traditional age of 60. The corpus calculation is similar but early retirement may have a longer retirement horizon, requiring a larger corpus.

faq.q: What are the challenges of early retirement in India?
faq.a: Challenges: longer retirement horizon (40+ years), higher inflation impact, no EPF/NPS top-up, health insurance costs without employer cover, social isolation, potential career regret. Also, corpus must grow through this period — conservative investments may not beat inflation.

faq.q: How do I calculate how much I need to retire at 45?
faq.a: 1. Estimate monthly expenses at age 45 (adjust for inflation). 2. Calculate total expenses from 45 to life expectancy (80–85). 3. Find present value of these expenses at post-retirement investment rate. This is your corpus target. Divide by your investment period to get monthly SIP needed.
No. Calculations run in your browser.
Yes. Works on all modern smartphones.

examples.heading: Retiring at 45 with ₹80,000/month expenses
examples.text: At 6% inflation, expenses at 45: ₹1,70,000/month (₹20.4 lakh/year). Retirement period: 40 years. Corpus needed at 7% post-retirement return: approximately ₹4.8 crore. Monthly SIP at 12% for 13 years: ₹1,50,000.

examples.heading: Impact of 5-year delay — retire at 50 vs 45
examples.text: Retire at 45: corpus needed ₹4.8 crore, monthly SIP ₹1,50,000 for 13 years. Retire at 50: corpus needed ₹5.2 crore (higher inflation-adjusted expenses), monthly SIP ₹90,000 for 18 years. Five extra working years dramatically reduce the monthly investment burden.
